video:video.video:title: Rome’s ‘sexy priest’ calendar star never set foot in a seminary
video:video.video:description: A calendar featuring close-ups of young, handsome priests has been a popular Rome souvenir for two decades. However, many of those photographed aren't actually priests.

video:video.video:title: Colorful procession of decorated elephants delights festival-goers in southern India
video:video.video:description: Crowds lined the streets in the southern Indian state of Kerala for a colourful elephant parade. The animals are an integral part of the state's religious traditions and rituals associated with temples and cultural festivities.
video:video.video:tag: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals

video:video.video:title: Firefighters extinguish flames after emergency service says Russian strike hit Sumy
video:video.video:description: Six people were injured in a Russian strike on Ukraine's northeastern city of Sumy on Thursday, the State Emergency Service of Ukraine said.

video:video.video:title: Heat wave intensifies in India as temperatures soar across northern plains
video:video.video:description: In India, the heat wave has intensified as temperatures soared across the northern plains on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Baby pygmy hippo takes first outdoor bath at Berlin Zoo
video:video.video:description: A baby pygmy hippopotamus at Berlin Zoo took its first outdoor bath this week after being born on May 9.

video:video.video:title: Meet the prehistoric predators that ruled the oceans in Jurassic times
video:video.video:description: A new exhibition at London’s Natural History Museum shines a light on Jurassic marine life and asks what their extinction could teach us about protecting the oceans now.

video:video.video:title: 'The Boys' ends: Stars on the spinoff and satire | AP interview
video:video.video:description: After five seasons of death, depravity and digs at the capitalistic superhero-industrial complex, “The Boys” has dropped its series finale.

video:video.video:title: Shakira opens up about her brightest and darkest moments
video:video.video:description: Shakira reflects on the brightest and darkest moments of her life. She'll perform alongside BTS and Madonna at the World Cup final halftime show in July.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese grandmother feeds thousands of compatriots who fled war
video:video.video:description: Soubhiye Zeiter started baking outside her tent in Beirut to feed herself and her family, displaced by war.

video:video.video:title: Fuerzas israelíes interceptan una flotilla que intenta romper el bloqueo a Gaza
video:video.video:description: Las fuerzas armadas israelíes interceptaron botes frente a la costa de Chipre este lunes, como parte de la más reciente ola de flotillas de activistas que intentan romper el bloqueo naval de Israel sobre Gaza.

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor announces affordable World Cup tickets to be made available for some lucky residents
video:video.video:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani announces 1,000 affordable tickets to upcoming World Cup matches to be made available to residents of New York.

video:video.video:title: Cannes jury members including Demi Moore attend handprint ceremony
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore and fellow 2026 Cannes Film Festival jury members have made their mark, joining jury president Park Chan-wook at a handprint ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Flotilla activists return to Italy and describe being beaten by Israeli forces
video:video.video:description: Two Italians activists, part of the Gaza Sumud Flotilla arrested by Israeli forces after being intercepted in international waters, returned to Italy on Thursday, describing their detention facility as “a place of terror.”

video:video.video:title: Russia continues large-scale nuclear drills
video:video.video:description: Russia on Thursday released a video showing large-scale maneuvers of its nuclear forces “to attain readiness for combat missions involving missile launches,” its Defense Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Blackpink's Jisoo appears at Dior event
video:video.video:description: Blackpink's Jisoo appeared before hundreds of fans at a Dior pop-up store event in Hong Kong, showcasing Jonathan Anderson's 2026 Fall collection. (May 21)

video:video.video:title: SpaceX reveals plans for record-breaking IPO that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk announced plans for one of the biggest stock sales ever by taking public a space company that is currently losing billions of dollars a year.

video:video.video:title: 'Love is the New Gangsta' for 6LACK | AP interview
video:video.video:description: For 6LACK, "family is what's cool, being vulnerable is what's cool." The Grammy-nominated rapper and singer-songwriter says "Love is the New Gangsta" represents his most personal — and honest — album to date.

video:video.video:title: Why meat-eating dinosaurs like T. rex evolved to have tiny arms
video:video.video:description: Why does such a big and powerful dinosaur like the T. rex have such tiny forearms, in a new study scientists believe they might have the answer.

video:video.video:title: This is how Cuba reacted to the US indictment of Raúl Castro
video:video.video:description: Cuban officials reacted angrily on Wednesday after U.S. prosecutors charged former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over the 1996 shootdown of two civilian planes operated by the Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue.

video:video.video:title: Zookeepers help raise baby aardvark
video:video.video:description: Keepers at the UK's Chester Zoo have welcomed a new arrival - a baby aardvark. The baby, named "Womble" was born to mother Oni on 3 April.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu's coalition alliances with religious parties put his reelection at risk
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has remained in power for the most of the past 17 years due in part to a tight alliance with ultra-Orthodox religious parties.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX reveals plans for what could be the biggest-ever initial public offering
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk announced plans Wednesday for one of the biggest sales of stock to the public ever for his space company that is currently losing billions of dollars a year.

video:video.video:title: Cantona on soccer and the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As he promotes a documentary about his life, former soccer star Eric Cantona gives his opinion on VAR and explains why he won't be watching the upcoming FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 21
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, May 21: Cuba condemns Raul Castro U.S. indictment; U.S. military says it boarded an Iranian-flagged tanker; First responders quarantined after suspected overdose deaths in New Mexico; SpaceX reveals plans for IPO.

video:video.video:title: NATO ministers comment on Trump's about face on US troop moves in Europe
video:video.video:description: Foreign ministers attending a NATO meeting in Helsingborg, Sweden, reacted Friday to U.S. President Donald Trump’s decision to send an additional 5,000 troops to Poland.

video:video.video:title: Sudan's war has left thousands missing. Many are buried in unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: It was a classic husband's phone call. He was done for the day and would stop at the market before coming home. But he was returning from war, not work.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out at hotel in Thailand's popular Pattaya area
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out at a hotel in Pattaya, Thailand, on Thursday, injuring four people, according to local media reports.

video:video.video:title: UK police seek witnesses in an inquiry into possible offenses by ex-Prince Andrew
video:video.video:description: British police are appealing for witnesses as they expand their investigation into potential offenses by Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Fans line up for Stephen Colbert’s final 'Late Show' taping
video:video.video:description: Fans lined up in the rain outside the Ed Sullivan Theater in New York on Thursday ahead of the final episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert.”

video:video.video:title: New York may ban flour used in thousands of pizzerias and bagel shops
video:video.video:description: Can New York Pizza and Bagels get better? A proposed additive ban could change New York’s pizza and bagels, some say for the better. (AP Video by David Martin)

video:video.video:title: Lizzo promises 'good, clean fun' on new album
video:video.video:description: Lizzo says her new album is "good, clean fun" as she walks the carpet at the starry amfAR gala outside the Cannes Film Festival. (May 21)

video:video.video:title: Ebola cases in eastern Congo’s Rwampara continue to arrive as treatment center is burned
video:video.video:description: People set fire to an Ebola treatment center in a town at the heart of the outbreak in eastern Congo on Thursday after being stopped from retrieving the body of a local man, a witness and a senior police officer said, as fear and anger grow over a health crisis that doctors are struggling to contain

video:video.video:title: Documents show Queen Elizabeth was eager for ex-Prince Andrew to become trade envoy
video:video.video:description: The late Queen Elizabeth II was “very keen” for former Prince Andrew to be named Britain’s trade envoy in 2001, according to documents released Thursday that showed his appointment received little scrutiny from government ministers.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of Cambodian villagers hold centuries-old ceremony
video:video.video:description: Villagers in Cambodia took part in a centuries-old ritual Thursday, honouring their village’s guardian spirits and praying for good fortune, rain and prosperity.

video:video.video:title: Trump and Putin's meetings with Chinese leader deliver markedly different messages
video:video.video:description: On the surface, Chinese leader Xi Jinping's back-to-back summits with U.S. President Donald Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin looked pretty similar, with formal handshakes in Beijing's Tiananmen Square, an enthusiastic greeting from flower-waving children, and marching columns of soldiers branching

video:video.video:title: Michigan Rep. Scholten pleased House republicans voted down women's museum bill
video:video.video:description: Hillary Scholten, a House Democrat from Michigan, says she's "pleased" that Republicans in Congress helped strike down a bill to build a Women's Museum after the GOP banned 'biological men' from exhibits.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Gregory Meeks said 'we had the votes' on Iran war resolution before House GOP called session off
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Gregory Meeks said "we had the votes" to compel President Donald Trump to withdraw from the war with Iran before House GOP delayed the session.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says Cuba has ‘always’ been a national security threat to the US
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Cuba has “always” been a national security threat to the United States.

video:video.video:title: After years of war, Sudan’s capital is left with thousands of unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: In Khartoum state, hastily buried bodies litter the city - buried in homes, in sports fields or by the road. Dr.

video:video.video:title: Sherlock Holmes enthusiasts reenact iconic duel at Switzerland’s Reichenbach Falls
video:video.video:description: From 221B Baker Street in central London to a cliffside waterfall in the Swiss Alps, generations of armchair detectives are celebrating International Sherlock Holmes Day.

video:video.video:title: After years of war, Sudan’s capital is left with thousands of unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: Three years of war have devastated much of Sudan, as The Associated Press' Fay Abuelgasim reports from the city of Omdurman, which sits on the western bank of the River Nile, opposite to the capital Khartoum.

video:video.video:title: French artist JR inflates a giant ‘cave’ over Paris’ oldest bridge
video:video.video:description: Paris’ oldest bridge has vanished. This week, the artist known as the “French Banksy,” JR, inflated a giant “cave” over the Pont Neuf — a monumental, rocky illusion swallowing the 17th-century landmark whole.

video:video.video:title: 2-time NASCAR champion Kyle Busch has died at 41, NASCAR says
video:video.video:description: Kyle Busch, a two-time Cup Series champion who won more races than anyone across NASCAR’s three national series, has died at 41.

video:video.video:title: Inflatable art turns The Hague into a giant open-air museum
video:video.video:description: The city center of The Hague has been transformed into a vibrant open-air museum with the arrival of inflatable art.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says US President Trump is "very disappointed at NATO right now”
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his latest mission to assuage nervous U.S. allies in Europe about the Trump administration’s intentions with NATO or at least put a friendlier face on whipsawing changes and uncertainty about American troop reductions.

video:video.video:title: Acting U.S. Navy secretary says some foreign military sales are being delayed
video:video.video:description: Taiwan hasn't been notified of any pause in a planned $14 billion U.S. arms sale to the self-governing island, a government official said Friday, after the acting U.S.

video:video.video:title: Everest record-holder Kami Rita Sherpa urges limit on climbers as crowds swell on the peak
video:video.video:description: Renowned Everest guide Kami Rita Sherpa urged authorities Friday to limit the number of climbers attempting to scale Mount Everest after reaching the summit for a record 32nd time this week.

video:video.video:title: Ebola treatment center burns in eastern Congo city of Rwampara
video:video.video:description: The Ebola treatment center in Rwampara in the Democratic Republic of Congo, burned Thursday as gunshots could be heard nearby and local residents were seen chasing away and hitting a white four-wheel drive vehicle often used by NGOs in the area.

video:video.video:title: Stress relief starts at the scalp: Japan-style head spas gain US fans
video:video.video:description: Head spas, a wellness trend that originated in Japan, are rapidly gaining popularity across the U.S., with hundreds of locations now offering scalp-focused treatments.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he doesn’t 'know' if he’s losing control of Senate Republicans
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump acknowledged he doesn’t "know” if he’s losing control of Senate Republicans, as GOP lawmakers appeared to abandon $1 billion in security funding for the White House ballroom complex and questions mounted over his administration’s $1.8 billion settlement fund.

video:video.video:title: Federal charges allege $90 million stolen from Minnesota social service programs
video:video.video:description: The new charges against fifteen people were announced as the former leader of a Minnesota nonprofit was sentenced to nearly 42 years in prison for her role in a $250 million fraud case.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, May 21st: Locals set fire to an Ebola treatment center in Congo; Buddhist hall in Japan burns to the ground; Flotilla members return to Italy after Israeli detainment; Temps soar to 113 Fahrenheit in India.

video:video.video:title: Buddhist hall housing 'eternal flame' burns down in Japan
video:video.video:description: A Buddhist hall in Western Japan known for housing an "eternal flame" that has been burning for over 1,000 years caught fire and burned to the ground on Miyajima Island.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee governor grants Tony Carruthers one-year stay after his execution was halted
video:video.video:description: Tennessee’s governor has granted a one-year stay of execution for Tony Carruthers after his lethal injection was halted when officials struggled to find a vein for more than an hour.

video:video.video:title: NYC residents could score $50 World Cup tickets through a new lottery
video:video.video:description: New York City residents will soon get a chance to buy $50 World Cup tickets through a city-run lottery, as officials try to make the tournament more accessible amid soaring ticket prices.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued after falling off cliff
video:video.video:description: A dog named Bailey was stranded after falling off a cliff top. In challenging conditions, a crew maneuvered a lifeboat as close as possible to the rocks before a crew member carefully entered the water and successfully rescued the dog.

video:video.video:title: $1 million bond set for livestreamer charged for allegedly shooting a man at Tennessee courthouse
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee judge has set a $1 million bond for a white livestreamer charged with attempted murder for allegedly shooting and wounding a Black man.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Athens after Israel intercepts international flotilla carrying Gaza activists
video:video.video:description: More than a thousand people gathered in the streets of Athens on Thursday to protest after Israeli forces intercepted an international flotilla carrying Gaza activists.

video:video.video:title: Saudi Arabia’s Interior Ministry opens its annual Hajj security parade ahead of pilgrimage season
video:video.video:description: Saudi Arabia’s Interior Ministry opens its annual Hajj security parade ahead of pilgrimage season

video:video.video:title: Trump's Washington arch design gets approval from key federal agency
video:video.video:description: A key federal agency has approved the design for an arch that President Donald Trump wants to build at an entrance to the nation's capital.

video:video.video:title: US raises pressure on Cuba with indictment of former leader
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors announced criminal charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro in the 1996 downing of civilian planes flown by Miami-based exiles as the Trump administration escalated pressure on the island’s socialist government.

video:video.video:title: Increased fuel costs have some Americans ready to scale back their Memorial Day travel plans
video:video.video:description: Higher fuel prices and other inflationary pressures are making most forms of travel more expensive as Memorial Day kicks off the summer season in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Atlantic hurricane season forecast to be milder than normal thanks to El Nino
video:video.video:description: Meteorologists predict a developing El Nino could dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won't eliminate storms.

video:video.video:title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Pedro Pascal is taking "Baby Yoda" to the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu." The star spoke with AP entertainment reporter Leslie Ambriz at the "Star Wars" film's red carpet premiere about sharing the suit of his helmeted Din Djarin character with stuntmen and body doubles Lateef Crowder and

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's postponing signing an executive order on AI
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called off a Thursday signing ceremony for a new order on artificial intelligence because he worried it could dull America’s edge on AI technology.

video:video.video:title: Mobile missile launchers play important role in US Pacific deterrence strategy
video:video.video:description: With a burst of flame, followed by a thunderclap boom that broke the bucolic serenity of the training area in the foothills of Japan’s Mount Fuji, the first rocket fired by the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Landmine blast kills 3 children in Syria, underscoring deadly legacy of war remnants
video:video.video:description: Idris al-Ridah wept as he carried the lifeless body of his 6-year-old son Amir in his arms, wrapped in a yellow and brown blanket.

video:video.video:title: Demi wows with dramatic looks in Cannes
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival is underway, with 12 days of nonstop premieres and fabulous red carpet looks from stars including Demi Moore, Jane Fonda and Diane Kruger. (May 15)

video:video.video:title: Soon-to-be graduates roll into their Michigan high school parking lot in tractors, ATVs and more
video:video.video:description: Dozens of soon-to-be graduates of a Michigan high school eschewed their cars and trucks and arrived in ATVs, golf carts and more on Tractor Day.

video:video.video:title: Nebraska officials say quarantined individuals doing well
video:video.video:description: Health Officials in Nebraska said Friday that passengers from the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak were doing well in quarantine at a hospital in Nebraska.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Rubio embarks on another mission to ease tensions with allies during NATO meeting
video:video.video:description: NATO allies and defense officials have expressed bewilderment at U.S. President Donald Trump’s announcement that he would send 5,000 U.S. troops to Poland.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Warsh sworn in as new Federal Reserve Chair
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump celebrated the White House swearing-in of the new Federal Reserve chair Friday, saying he wants Kevin Warsh’s help stimulating the economy while insisting his hand-picked chair and the central bank will remain independent.

video:video.video:title: How to win the red carpet at Cannes
video:video.video:description: As 12 days of non-stop premieres at the Cannes Film Festival come to an end, here's how to be successfully styled on the famous red carpet. (May 22)

video:video.video:title: Rubio says U.S. force posture in Europe will eventually be reduced
video:video.video:description: Rubio says America’s NATO allies understand that eventually there will be a reduction in the U.S. troop presence in Europe as the Trump administration evaluates its force posture globally.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief gives update on Ebola epidemic in Congo and Uganda
video:video.video:description: The head of the World Health Organization said Friday that the Ebola outbreak in Congo is rapidly spreading and now poses a “very high” risk at the national level.

video:video.video:title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump’s national intelligence director
video:video.video:description: Gabbard cited her husband's health as a reason for her departure but it comes weeks after she repeatedly dodged questions in a congressional hearing about whether the White House had been warned of potential fallout from the Iran conflict.

video:video.video:title: NATO allies bewildered by Trump saying he's sending 5,000 troops to Poland
video:video.video:description: President Trump on Thursday said the U.S. will send an additional 5,000 troops to Poland, stirring confusion following weeks of changing statements from Trump and his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.

video:video.video:title: AP explains concerns about the Ebola epidemic
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization said that the outbreak now poses a “very high” risk for Congo — up from a previous categorization of “high” — but that the risk of the disease spreading globally remains low.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: SpaceX rocket ignites into flames after landing in the Indian Ocean
video:video.video:description: SpaceX's Starship reached it's final destination — the Indian Ocean — despite some engine trouble, before erupting in flames upon impact.

video:video.video:title: 911 call reveals more about Kyle Busch's condition before death
video:video.video:description: NASCAR driver Kyle Busch experienced shortness of breath, felt he was overheating and was coughing up blood the day before his death, according to a 911 call obtained Friday by The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Kyle Busch on New York 'victory lap' after 2007 NASCAR champions week
video:video.video:description: Kyle Busch, a two-time Cup Series champion who won more races than anyone across NASCAR’s three national series, has died. He was 41.

video:video.video:title: Advocates celebrate 230th anniversary of Ona Judge's escape from enslavement by George Washington
video:video.video:description: Ona Judge was 22 when she escaped from the president's Philadelphia residence on May 21, 1796. An exhibition about her and other enslaved people at Independence Mall, ordered removed by the Trump administration, has not been fully restored.

video:video.video:title: Reporter covering Groundhog Day in 1992 meets Bill Murray
video:video.video:description: Reporter Ted Anthony describes meeting Bill Murray while covering Groundhog Day in 1992.

video:video.video:title: WHO official warns Ebola outbreak in DRC is spreading rapidly as suspected cases rise
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization warned Friday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading rapidly, as officials race to contain the virus amid insecurity and population displacement in the country’s east.

video:video.video:title: Cuba rallies support for Raúl Castro after U.S. brings criminal charges
video:video.video:description: The Cuban government called for a rally in Havana on Friday in support of Raúl Castro, one of the leaders of the Cuban Revolution.

video:video.video:title: 'La Bola Negra' directors call Glenn Close during their interview
video:video.video:description: "La Bola Negra" star Glenn Close couldn't make the red carpet premiere in Cannes last night and so directors Javier Calvo and Javier Ambrossi called during an interview with The Associated Press to inform her on the movie's success.

video:video.video:title: US indictment puts Raúl Castro back in the spotlight
video:video.video:description: Raúl Castro is back at the center of U.S.-Cuba tensions after a U.S. indictment accused him in the 1996 shootdown of exile planes.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 22nd: U.S. National Intelligence Director Tulsi Gabbard resigns; NATO allies confused by Trump ordering troops to Poland; 1.6 million Muslim pilgrims arrive in Saudi Arabia; and Cubans rally for former President Raúl Castro

video:video.video:title: Sherlock Holmes fans celebrate the world’s most famous fictional detective
video:video.video:description: Fans around the world marked International Sherlock Holmes Day by celebrating the legendary detective created by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle — with some even traveling to Switzerland’s Reichenbach Falls to reenact Holmes’ famous showdown with Professor Moriarty.

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus receives star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Miley Cyrus says her Hollywood Walk of Fame star represents "an accumulation of devotion" and hopes her art "awakens something raw and imperfect and sexy and glamorous and joyful in times that need it."

video:video.video:title: In violent Guayaquil, Ecuador's young players train behind guards as World Cup hope rises
video:video.video:description: As a child, Piero Hicapié — one of the stars of the Ecuadorian national team — spent a brief stint in one of Barcelona’s youth academies.

video:video.video:title: Judge dismisses criminal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported
video:video.video:description: A federal judge has dismissed a human smuggling case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, finding that the Justice Department’s pursuit of criminal charges was designed to punish him for challenging his mistaken deportation to El Salvador last year.

video:video.video:title: DRCongo struggles to isolate Ebola cases as outbreak continues to spread
video:video.video:description: An incident manager on the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s national Ebola response team said on Friday that suspected Ebola patients have mixed with the general population at the region’s general hospitals.

video:video.video:title: Four killed, 39 injured after Ukrainian drone strike on dormitory in Russian-occupied Starobilsk
video:video.video:description: The death toll from a Ukrainian drone strike on a college dormitory building in Starobilsk, a city in Ukraine’s Russian-occupied Luhansk region, has risen to six, while 15 people remain missing, Russian President Vladimir Putin said Friday.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX launches biggest Starship yet on a test flight
video:video.video:description: SpaceX launched its biggest, most powerful Starship yet on a test flight Friday, an upgraded version that NASA is counting on to land astronauts on the moon.

video:video.video:title: NASCAR CEO talks about Kyle Busch at news conference before race
video:video.video:description: NASCAR CEO Steve O’Donnell and driver Christopher Bell share stories about Kyle Busch ahead of a weekend racing event at the Charlotte Motor Speedway in North Carolina.

video:video.video:title: Cambodia releases ten endangered crocodiles into river
video:video.video:description: Conservationists in Cambodia released ten critically endangered Siamese crocodiles into a river on Friday in a multi-organization species recovery effort.

video:video.video:title: Senior Buddhist monk accused of child sexual abuse released on bail in Sri Lanka
video:video.video:description: A Sri Lankan court on Friday released on bail a senior Buddhist monk who was arrested on suspicion of sexually abusing a minor.

video:video.video:title: Rubio arrives in India ahead of Quad talks as US tries to reset strained ties
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday ahead of a meeting next week with his counterparts from India, Australia, and Japan - members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance known as the Quad.

video:video.video:title: Japanese woman who scaled the world's 14 top peaks wants to share joy
video:video.video:description: The first woman to have three times conquered the world's second-highest peak, K2, has also summited the world's 14 highest peaks.

video:video.video:title: Danish recycling artist brings fairy tale-inspired creations to his first museum exhibit
video:video.video:description: Danish recycling artist Thomas Dambo is renown for his wooden troll sculptures that are usually hidden away in unlikely places waiting to be found by his troll hunter fans around the world.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of flotilla activists land in Turkey, recount abuse and torture by Israeli authorities
video:video.video:description: Wearing grey sweatsuits and Arab keffiyehs, the activists descended the stairs to the runaway in Istanbul flashing two-fingered salutes and chanting “Free Palestine.”
